title: groupAggregate — groupAggregate
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ a <- new.magpie(cells_and_regions = c("ZAF.1","ZAR.2","VEN.3"),                 years = c("y1995","y2005"),                  names = c("Wheat","Barley","Sugar_cane","Sugar_beet"),                  fill=1:30)  #> a #An object of class "magpie" #, , Wheat # #      y1995 y2005 #ZAF.1     1     4 #ZAR.2     2     5 #VEN.3     3     6 # #, , Barley # #      y1995 y2005 #ZAF.1     7    10 #ZAR.2     8    11 #VEN.3     9    12 # #, , Sugar_cane # #      y1995 y2005 #ZAF.1    13    16 #ZAR.2    14    17 #VEN.3    15    18 # #, , Sugar_beet # #      y1995 y2005 #ZAF.1    19    22 #ZAR.2    20    23 #VEN.3    21    24  b<-groupAggregate(a,dim=3) #> b #An object of class "magpie" #, , tece # #      y1995 y2005 #ZAF.1     8    14 #ZAR.2    10    16 #VEN.3    12    18 # #, , sugr_cane # #      y1995 y2005 #ZAF.1    13    16 #ZAR.2    14    17 #VEN.3    15    18 # #, , sugr_beet # #      y1995 y2005 #ZAF.1    19    22 #ZAR.2    20    23 #VEN.3    21    24  c<-groupAggregate(a,dim=1) #> c #An object of class "magpie" #, , Wheat # #      y1995 y2005 #AFR.1     3     9 #LAM.2     3     6 # #, , Barley # #      y1995 y2005 #AFR.1    15    21 #LAM.2     9    12 # #, , Sugar_cane # #      y1995 y2005 #AFR.1    27    33 #LAM.2    15    18 # #, , Sugar_beet # #      y1995 y2005 #AFR.1    39    45 #LAM.2    21    24  } # }

title: Interpolate 2 — interpolate2
text: disaggregated MAgPIE object containing x_ini first timestep

title: Interpolate 2 — interpolate2
text: now deleted function called interpolate package , hence name interpolate2. function based following assumption: x object low resolution one data dimension sum data dimensions constant time. Example: One column cropland, one (cell size - cropland). x_ini provides type data x high resolution time step previous initial time step x (e.g. x goes t=t=+10, x_ini must provided t=-1). function calculates amount individual data columns x change timestep. output based x_ini differences later timesteps ths starting point disaggregated given mapping. assures little information possible lost original dataset x_ini. disaggregation procedure works follows: 1. Differences distribution years derived low resolution data set. 2. Based differences extension reduction shares calculated different pools. Reduction shares calculated relative pool (e.g. reduction cropland pool 10ha 6ha leads reduction share (10ha-6ha)/10ha = 40%). time extension shares calculated relative pool made available reductions pools (e.g. cropland reduced 10ha 6ha, forest area reduced 2ha 1ha, pastureland increases 20ha 25ha. case extension share pasture (25ha-20ha)/(10ha-6ha+2ha-1ha)=5ha/5ha=100%). difference calculation reduction extension share crucial application high resolution level otherwise calculation add . 3. Reduction extension shares disaggregated high resolution level just assigning low resolution shares belonging cells higher resolution. 4.Starting provided high resolution pool data set initial year reduction shares applied pools cells. pool made available expansions calculated summing values released pool reductions. 5. Pool expansions calculated based pool made available 4 first time step. 6. Steps 4 5 repeated following years based newly created high resolution data. Applying procedure makes sure relative pool reductions identical low resolution cell belonging high resolutions cells whereas extension shares relative areas made available per cell identical low resolution cell belonging high resolution cells.

title: Interpolate 2 — interpolate2
text: Jan Philipp Dietrich

title: interpolateAvlCroplandWeighted — interpolateAvlCroplandWeighted
text: x Time series land pools (model output) disaggregated. x_ini_lr low resolution distribution x model optimization. x_ini_hr initial high resolution distribution x (land-use initialisation) model optimization. avl_cropland_hr area available cropland high resolution. map relation map low high resolution, path data.frame urban_land_hr Either magpie object cellular urban input data, "static" string marginal_land Depending cropland suitability data, standard options "all_marginal": Cropland can allocated marginal land "q33_marginal": bottom tertile marginal land area excluded "no_marginal": Marginal land fully excluded cropland land_consv_hr magpie object containing conservation land, e.g. cell.conservation_land_0.5.mz output folder peat_hr Disaggregated peatland MAgPIE peatland pools snv_pol_shr Share available cropland witheld land cover types. Can supplied single value magpie object containing different values iso country. snv_pol_fader Fader share set aside policy. year_ini Timestep assumed initial distributions x_ini_hr x_ini_lr. unit Unit output. "Mha", "ha" "share"

title: interpolateAvlCroplandWeighted — interpolateAvlCroplandWeighted
text: disaggregated MAgPIE object containing x_ini_hr first timestep

title: interpolateAvlCroplandWeighted — interpolateAvlCroplandWeighted
text: function requires following input data: x object containing time series land pools (model output). sum land pools constant time. x_ini_lr x_ini_hr provide initial land pools (Mha) high (hr) low resolution (lr) optimisation. contain initial time step, share three-dimensional structure x. avl_cropland_hr provides information amount (Mha) available cropland high resolution. map relation map containing information cell belongings high low resolution. weighted disaggregation works follows: 1. share cropland terms total available cropland calculated previous time step multiplied available cropland current time step (available cropland can change time - e.g. policy restriction can specified snv_pol_shr). temporary cropland pool compared low resolution cropland pool residual area cropland expansion reduction determined. 2. order allocate residual area cropland expansion reduction, grid cell high resolution expansion reduction weights calculated multiplied residual area: reduction weight given ratio amount cropland per grid cell total area temporary cropland low resolution spatial unit. assumes cropland reduction equally distributed among high resolution grid cells. expansion weight calculated ratio remaining cropland grid cell level (high resolution) overall remaining cropland low resolution spatial unit current time step. remaining cropland given difference available cropland temporaryl cropland pool minus urban land, since assumed cropland allocated urban land. 3. Following cropland allocation, land area remaining non-cropland vegetation pools calculated substracting allocated cropland urban land areas total land area grid cell. 4. non-cropland vegetation pool high resolution (except primary forest), calculated step 3., multiplied respective shares remaining non-cropland vegetation pools previous time step (temporary allocation). Similar cropland allocation, sufficient also account changes within land pools. Therefore, temporarily allocated non-cropland pools , , compared pools low resolution. residual area land expansion reduction allocated based reduction expansion weights, similar 2.. reduction weight calculated ratio given temporary land pool high resolution total temporary land pool low resolution. expansion weight calculated ratio remaining land filled land pool total amount residual land allocated current time step. 5. Primary forest treated slightly different way, primary forest expanded time. cropland cells cropland expansion, primary forest , first, assumed remain constant transferred previous time step current time step. , sum temporarary allocation compared sum primary forest low resolution determine residual primary forest land, still needs allocated. surplus primary forest, reduction weight calculated similarly 5., land area reduced accordingly. areas temprorily allocated primary forest falls short, allocation weight calculated function difference primary land previous time step current time step. makes sure expansion primary forest. 7. Urban land assumed constant time.

title: interpolateAvlCroplandWeighted — interpolateAvlCroplandWeighted
text: Patrick von Jeetze, David Chen

title: interpolateAvlCroplandWeighted — interpolateAvlCroplandWeighted
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ a <- interpolateAvlCroplandWeighted(   x = land,   x_ini_lr = land_ini_lr,   x_ini_hr = land_ini_hr,   avl_cropland_hr = "avl_cropland_0.5.mz",   map = "clustermap_rev4.59_c200_h12.rds",   marginal_land = "all_marginal" )  sf <- read.magpie("f30_scenario_fader.csv")[, , "by2030"]  b <- interpolateAvlCroplandWeighted(   x = land,   x_ini_lr = land_ini_lr,   x_ini_hr = land_ini_hr,   avl_cropland_hr = "avl_cropland_0.5.mz",   map = "clustermap_rev4.59_c200_h12.rds",   marginal_land = "all_marginal",   snv_pol_shr = 0.2,   snv_pol_fader = sf )  iso <- readGDX(gdx, "iso") set_aside_iso <- readGDX(gdx, "policy_countries30") set_aside_select <- readGDX(gdx, "s30_snv_shr") set_aside_noselect <- readGDX(gdx, "s30_snv_shr_noselect") snv_pol_shr <- new.magpie(iso, fill = snv_noselect) snv_pol_shr[set_aside_iso, , ] <- set_aside_select  c <- interpolateAvlCroplandWeighted(   x = land,   x_ini_lr = land_ini_lr,   x_ini_hr = land_ini_hr,   avl_cropland_hr = "avl_cropland_0.5.mz",   map = "clustermap_rev4.59_c200_h12.rds",   marginal_land = "all_marginal",   snv_pol_shr = snv_pol_shr,   snv_pol_fader = saf ) } # }

title: luqueries — luqueries
text: Standardqueries: spatial: iso_reg: ISO_3166_1_alpha_3 country code MAgPIE regions code fao_iso: faostat country name ISO_3166_1_alpha_3 country code fbs_iso: similar fao_iso, slightly different names used food balance sheets goods: faostat_kcr: faostat product names crops MAgPIE crop product categories faostat_kli: faostat product names crops MAgPIE livestock product categories query made script "luquery.R" SVN folder tools/queries. Please use script extend query. Please use NAs queries, scripts might problems handling .

title: Speed Aggregate — speed_aggregate
text: Aggregates MAgPIE object one resolution another based relation matrix

title: Speed Aggregate — speed_aggregate
text: x MAgPIE object file name MAgPIE object rel relation matrix, file name spam relation matrix (".spam" file ending), file name region mapping (".csv" file ending, ";" used separator 3 columns: Country Name, Country code region code) fname file name file output written . fname=NULL aggregated data returned function weight MAgPIE object containing weights considered weighted aggregation. Please see "details" section information Name first column used rel region mapping (set first second column used). setting ignored spam files. Name second column used rel region mapping (set second third column used). setting ignored spam files. dim Specifiying dimension MAgPIE object (dis-)aggregated. Either specified integer (1=spatial,2=temporal,3=data) want specify subdimension specified name dimension position within given dimension (e.g. 3.2 means 2nd data dimension, 3.8 means 8th data dimension). partrel set TRUE allowes relation matrix contain less entries x vice versa. values without relation lost output.

title: Speed Aggregate — speed_aggregate
text: fname=NULL, aggregated data, otherwise nothing returned.

title: Speed Aggregate — speed_aggregate
text: Basically speed_aggregate nothing normal matrix multiplication taking account 3 dimensional structure MAgPIE objects. , can provide kind relation matrix like. However, easier usability also possible provide weights weighted (dis-)aggregation MAgPIE object. case rel must 1-0-matrix containing mapping resolutions. weight needs provided higher spatial aggregation, meaning aggregation spatial resolution input data case dissaggregation spatial resolution output data. temporal data dimension must either identical resolution data set (dis-)aggregated 1. temporal /data dimension 1 means transformation matrix applied years /data columns. case column just summed instead calculated weighted average either provide weight (columns just summed ) set specific weighting column NA.

title: Speed Aggregate — speed_aggregate
text: Jan Philipp Dietrich, Ulrich Kreidenweis

title: superAggregate — superAggregate
text: Function applies aggregation functions subset magpie lpj object.

title: superAggregate — superAggregate
code: superAggregate(data, aggr_type, level="reg", weight=NULL, na.rm=TRUE, crop_aggr=FALSE, ...)

title: superAggregate — superAggregate
text: data MAgPIE LPJ object aggr_type Aggregation Type. Can function one two objects (data weight) size. Currently pre-supported functions: "sum","mean","weighted_mean". level Aggregation level: Either level type (name) vector 3-Character region names length data cells. Allowed level types global "glo", regional "reg", per Country "country" per REMIND regions "remind_reg".  "country" "remind_reg" supported 0.5 grid datacells 59199 cells always returned arrays. use vector regions aggregation take place according regions. weight Currently used weighted_mean (see weighted.mean, yet also applicable individualized functions. size data. na.rm TRUE, NAs ignored data weight. crop_aggr determines whether output crop-specific (FALSE) aggregated crops (TRUE). method used aggregation set aggr_type (Currently works levels "reg" "glo") ... additional arguments aggregation method standard functions (self-created ones)

title: superAggregate — superAggregate
text: case level="glo" "reg", function returns MAgPIE object. case level="country" array returned. case LPJ object aggregated, list MAgPIE objects returned, entry one 4th dimension slices.

title: superAggregate — superAggregate
text: Benjamin Bodirsky, Jan Philipp Dietrich, Florian Humpenoeder
